text: Interactive Brokers, the Fourth-Largest U.S. Brokerage, Adds Nine Crypto Tokens and Two-Way Stablecoin Transfers Interactive Brokers has added trading support for nine tokens through zerohash: AAVE, APT, CC, LDO, MON, NEAR, XPL, PAXG and UNI. The fourth-largest U.S. brokerage has also added AAVE, UNI and PAXG through Paxos. In addition, the platform has launched two-way stablecoin transfers, allowing clients to automatically convert U.S. dollars held in their IBKR accounts into USDC, PYUSD or RLUSD and send the funds to external wallets. Eligible clients can also transfer supported digital assets between IBKR and custodial or non-custodial wallets. — link

text: UK and US Seek Stablecoin Regulatory Convergence and Cross-Border Market Access The UK and US governments issued a joint statement supporting closer coordination on stablecoin regulation and their use in cross-border payments, settlement and capital markets. Stablecoins held out as money should be backed at least 1:1 by high-quality liquid assets. Reserves should be segregated from issuers' own funds, with timely redemption and a clear, protected claim for holders in the event of insolvency. Both governments intend to pursue comparable regulatory outcomes for comparable risks and activities, while avoiding reserve and prudential requirements that are disproportionate to risk or create barriers to competition. They also support fair, risk-based access to financial services and markets for regulated stablecoin providers, and plan to explore a clear pathway for stablecoins issued in either jurisdiction to access the other market. — link

text: Review: Major Institutions' Bitcoin Bottom Price Predictions for This Cycle Major institutional assessments of Bitcoin's cycle bottom cluster mainly in two ranges: $50,000–$60,000 and $40,000–$46,000. However, some figures are base-case bottom forecasts, while others represent valuation floors, support levels or bearish scenarios. Standard Chartered said $59,000 may have marked the bottom; CryptoQuant, NYDIG and Citi identified key levels near $53,000–$54,000; 10x Research's latest model pointed to $46,628–$50,732; and Galaxy Research placed its base-case bottom at $40,000–$46,000. Bitfinex and 22V Research also identified potential downside toward $40,000 under weaker demand or a decisive support break, while forecasts below $40,000 mostly reflect prolonged bear-market, recession or severe stress scenarios. Forecasts from industry figures are more dispersed, ranging from around $57,000 to below $30,000. Overall, there is no unified institutional consensus that Bitcoin will bottom at $44,000–$46,000. — link

text: A Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age > Demis Hassabis argues AGI could be one of humanity's most transformative technologies. > "The magnitude of this technology's impact will be unprecedented, perhaps 10x of the Industrial Revolution at 10x the speed." > However, he warns that accelerating frontier AI requires stronger safeguards, international coordination, and responsible deployment to manage cyber, bio, and other emerging risks. > He proposes a U.S-led Frontier AI Standards Body to evaluate advanced models, set evolving safety benchmarks, audit frontier labs, and establish a global framework for safe AGI development while preserving innovation. text: how likely do you think a cashcat listing on RH is in the next 2 months? me personally, i would say sub 20% chance. i just don't see it. why would they list now and make their native userbase EL for on-chainers? i get the argument that they wanna maximize attention for RH chain, but if we go by this logic then why fire the biggest bullet they have right at the start? would make sense to wait and try every other thing first and then list it as a crescendo. thinking it will get eventually listed but more than 2 months out. curious to hear some other opinions. as of now, i am sidelined. will adjust stance and slam buys if they list (or tweet smth meaningful). if they don't do that soon then i think marginal buyer is in as of now.

text: Upbit to List Derive's DRV as Former Lyra Token Enters Korean Market Upbit, South Korea's largest crypto exchange, will list Derive (DRV) with KRW, BTC and USDT trading pairs, with trading scheduled to begin at 17:00 Korea time on July 14. Derive, formerly known as the onchain options protocol Lyra Finance, rebranded in 2024 and migrated its token from LYRA to DRV. The protocol currently focuses on onchain options and perpetual futures trading. — link

author: admin#1742094092

text: Korea Stock Rout Deepens as Upbit Volume Surges 1,426% South Korea's KOSPI extended its sharp selloff on July 14, falling 4% intraday to 6,534.34, with SK Hynix down more than 7%. At the same time, Upbit, South Korea's largest crypto exchange, saw its 24-hour trading volume rise to USD 4.27 billion, up 1,426.2% from the previous day, according to CoinGecko. The Korea Economic Daily reported that forced liquidations triggered by unsettled stock trades reached KRW 425.8 billion, or about USD 286 million, from July 1 to July 10, including KRW 142.2 billion on July 9 and another KRW 81.6 billion on July 10. — link

text: Binance US Plots Comeback With 20% U.S. Market Share Target Binance US CEO Stephen Gregory said the company is restarting growth after a two-year regulatory setback and plans to reclaim about 20% of the U.S. crypto trading market. Binance US, the U.S. affiliate operating under the Binance brand, has cut fees close to zero, with a 0% maker fee, and is using incentives to rebuild liquidity. Gregory said that as the U.S. regulatory environment improves, Binance US will seek additional licenses and expand into derivatives, perpetual futures and prediction markets, putting it in more direct competition with Coinbase and Kraken for U.S. users. — link

text: U.S.-Linked Wallets Move Nearly $297 Million in Seized Crypto to Coinbase Prime According to Arkham data, a wallet linked to the U.S. government that had been dormant for about four years transferred 140.214 BTC, worth about USD 8.78 million, to a Coinbase Prime cold wallet at 23:52 Beijing time on July 13. Four hours earlier, U.S.-linked addresses also moved 2,875 BTC and 925.513 BTC, worth a combined USD 235 million, with the funds tied to seized assets from the Ryan Farace and BTC-e cases. Another 30,007 ETH linked to the Brian Krewson case was also transferred to Coinbase Prime, worth about USD 53.09 million. — link

text: Binance Releases 44th Proof of Reserves Report Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange by user count and trading volume, released its 44th Proof of Reserves report with a July 1 snapshot. User BTC holdings rose 1.22% from June 1 to about 640,000 BTC, an increase of 7,715 BTC, while user ETH holdings fell 1.41% to about 4.08 million ETH, a decrease of 58,591 ETH. User USDT holdings fell 1.51% to about 33.7 billion USDT, a decrease of roughly 510 million USDT. — link
